* McsEngl.VscCmd.Next-Match: enter,
* McsEngl.VscCmd.enter: Next-Match,
* McsEngl.VscCmds.enter: Next-Match,

VscCmd.custom

description::
× Mcsh-creation: {2026-04-02},
· MY customiioo commands.

* ctrl+alt+-, // selected-number dicrease
* ctrl+alt+=, // selected-number increase

=== CHAR:
* ctrl+alt+c h l, // char Html LeshThan: & lt;
* ctrl+alt+c h s, // char Html space: & nbsp;
* ctrl+alt+c i, // char isert from Integer EXTENSION
* ctrl+alt+c p b, // char hitP insert line Break: " <br> "
* ctrl+alt+c p e, // char hitP name Equal: !=
* ctrl+alt+c p m, // char hitP name Main: !⇒
* ctrl+alt+c p s, // char hitP sentence: " <br>· "

=== HTML
* ctrl+alt+h b // Html Bold
* ctrl+alt+h c d // Html CoDe: >code>
* ctrl+alt+h c m // Html CoMment: >!-- -->
* ctrl+alt+h l i, // Html Link Insert
* ctrl+alt+h l s, // Html Link Selection
* ctrl+alt+h t // Html Tag insert EXTENSION

=== MACRO
* ctrl+alt+m c, // macro Copy recording
* ctrl+alt+m d, // macro insert date: {2026-03-30}
* ctrl+alt+m escape, // macro stop playback
* ctrl+alt+m p, // macro Cancel recording
* ctrl+alt+m p, // macro Playback
* ctrl+alt+m r, // macro recording
* ctrl+alt+m r, // macro stop recording

=== HITP:
=== HITP COLOR:
* ctrl+alt+p c b, // hitP Color Blue
* ctrl+alt+p c g, // hitP Color Green
* ctrl+alt+p c r, // hitP Color Red
=== HITP FORMAT:
* ctrl+alt+p f u, // hitP Format Underline
=== HITP ELEMENT:
* ctrl+alt+p e a, // hitP Element Audio
* ctrl+alt+p h =, // hitP Heading Increase
* ctrl+alt+p h -, // hitP Heading Decrease
=== HITP LINK:
* ctrl+alt+p l i, // hitP Linkpreview Insert
* ctrl+alt+p l s, // hitP Linkpreview Selection
=== HITP NAME:
* ctrl+alt+p n a l, // hitP name ALbanian
* ctrl+alt+p n a r, // hitP name ARabic
* ctrl+alt+p n c, // hitP name Chinese
* ctrl+alt+p n e, // hitP name Esperanto
* ctrl+alt+p n f, // hitP name Frence
* ctrl+alt+p n g a, // hitP name GreekAncient
* ctrl+alt+p n g k, // hitP name GreeK
* ctrl+alt+p n g r, // hitP name GeRman
* ctrl+alt+p n h, // hitP name Hindi
* ctrl+alt+p n i, // hitP name Italian
* ctrl+alt+p n j, // hitP name Japanese
* ctrl+alt+p n k h, // hitP name KHmer
* ctrl+alt+p n k r, // hitP name KoRean
* ctrl+alt+p n r, // hitP name Romanean
* ctrl+alt+p n s b, // Bosnian-Croatian-Montenegrin-Serbian
* ctrl+alt+p n s l, // hitP name SlavoBuLgarian
* ctrl+alt+p n s m, // hitP name SlavoMacedonian
* ctrl+alt+p n s n, // hitP name SiNagu
* ctrl+alt+p n s p, // hitP name SPanish
* ctrl+alt+p n s r, // hitP name SlavoRussian
* ctrl+alt+p n s s, // hitP name SlavoSlovenian
* ctrl+alt+p n t, // hitP name Turkish
* ctrl+alt+p n v, // hitP name Vietnamese
=== HITP PARAGRAPH:
* ctrl+alt+p p d, // hitP Paragraph Description
* ctrl+alt+p p n, // hitP Paragraph Name
* ctrl+alt+p p p, // hitP Paragraph Description and Name
=== HITP RESOURCE:
* ctrl+alt+p r r, // hitP insert Resource Resource
* ctrl+alt+p r s, // hitP insert Resource hmnSngu
=== HITP SYNTAX:
* ctrl+alt+p s a, // hitP argument-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s c, // hitP cause-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s d, // hitP direction-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s f, // hitP frequency-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s k, // hitP subject-complement-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s l e, // hitP syntax language Elln-Greek
* ctrl+alt+p s m, // hitP manner-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s o, // hitP object-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s p, // hitP sPace-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s q, // hitP quantity-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s s, // hitP subject-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s t, // hitP time-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s u, // hitP oUtput-syntax
* ctrl+alt+p s v, // hitP verb-syntax
=== HITP SAVE:
* ctrl+alt+p v 2, // hitP saVe 2 minor versioning

* ctrl+alt+t,
* ctrl+alt+v, // shows char codepoint in different formats
* F6, // misc macro

name::
* McsEngl.VscCmd.custom!⇒VscCmdc,
* McsEngl.VscCmdc!=VscCmd.custom,

relation-to-Atom of Vscode

description::
× Mcsh-creation: {2025-12-11},
"Below is a clear, business-style explanation of the relationship between **Atom** and **Visual Studio Code (VS Code)**, including their history, shared technology layers, and ecosystem implications.
---
## 1. High-Level Relationship
**Atom and VS Code are unrelated products built by different companies**, but they are connected indirectly because **both were built on top of the same underlying framework: Electron**.
* **Atom** was developed by **GitHub** (launched 2014).
* **VS Code** was developed by **Microsoft** (launched 2015).
* Both applications run on **Electron**, a framework originally created by GitHub to build desktop apps with web technologies.
---
## 2. Shared Foundation: Electron
Atom was the **first major application** built on the original Electron codebase (then called Atom Shell).
Microsoft later adopted Electron to build VS Code.
This creates the appearance of a relationship, but the editors themselves were developed entirely separately.
Key implications:
* Similar UI technology stack (HTML, CSS, JavaScript).
* Cross-platform behavior is consistent across both.
* Extensions in both systems often leveraged Node.js packages.
---
## 3. Development Philosophy Differences
Although they share a foundation, their **editor philosophies diverged significantly**.
### **Atom**
* Designed as a fully hackable, modular text editor.
* Deep customization through plugins and user configuration.
* Performance was sometimes criticized due to heavy customization layers.
### **VS Code**
* Focused on performance, developer productivity, and integrations.
* Strong built-in tooling for debugging, terminals, Git, and language servers.
* Opinionated but extensible marketplace.
---
## 4. Acquisition and Strategic Shift
In **2018**, Microsoft acquired **GitHub**.
After the acquisition:
* Microsoft continued investing in VS Code, not Atom.
* Atom’s development slowed.
* In **June 2022**, GitHub officially discontinued Atom and its associated packages.
Because Microsoft already owned VS Code, continuing Atom did not make strategic sense.
---
## 5. Impact on Users and Ecosystem
* **Atom is deprecated**, no longer maintained.
* Many Atom users migrated to **VS Code**, which offered better performance and a larger extension library.
* Some community-driven Atom forks (e.g., Pulsar) emerged to keep the ecosystem alive.
---
## Summary
| Aspect | Atom | VS Code |
| Origin | GitHub | Microsoft |
| Year released | 2014 | 2015 |
| Technology | Electron | Electron |
| Philosophy | Hackable editor | Fast, productivity-focused IDE-like editor |
| Status | Discontinued (2022) | Actively maintained |
**Relationship:**
Atom and VS Code share a common technical foundation (Electron), but they were developed independently and diverged in performance, adoption, and strategic relevance. Microsoft’s acquisition of GitHub ended Atom’s development in favor of VS Code.
---
If you want, I can also provide:
* A short historical timeline.
* A comparison of technical architectures.
* A migration guide from Atom packages/workflows to VS Code equivalents."
[{2025-12-10 retrieved} https://chatgpt.com/c/69399ae0-d638-8329-bd8d-c7dae3a8b52b]

SEARCH::
· this page uses 'locator-names', names that when you find them, you find the-LOCATION of the-concept they denote.
⊛ GLOBAL-SEARCH:
· clicking on the-green-BAR of a-page you have access to the-global--locator-names of my-site.
· use the-prefix 'Vscode' for senso-concepts related to current concept 'Visual-Studio-Code'.
⊛ LOCAL-SEARCH:
· TYPE CTRL+F "McsLang.words-of-concept's-name", to go to the-LOCATION of the-concept.
· a-preview of the-description of a-global-name makes reading fast.
